title: NOC 53125 – Patternmakers – Textile, Leather and Fur Products | GTR Immigration
description: Precision and technical skill define the work of NOC 53125. Patternmakers are the essential link between a designer's sketch and a finished garment or product. As Canada’s high-end manufacturing and boutique fashion sectors grow, the demand for these TEER 3 technical experts is rising. This code is vital for the operational success of major Canadian brands in Vancouver, Toronto, and Montreal.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category5 – Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sportTEER Level3Major Group53 – Occupations in art, culture and sportSub-Major Group531 – Occupations in art and cultureMinor Group5312 – Occupations in creative and performing artsNOC Code53125

duties: Create master patterns for garments, leather goods, or fur products.
Use computer-aided design (CAD) software to develop and grade patterns.
Calculate material requirements and optimize fabric usage.
Coordinate with designers and production managers to ensure design feasibility.
Examine and adjust patterns to ensure a perfect fit for samples.
employment_requirements: Completion of a college or other program in pattern making OR several years of experience as a tailor or seamstress is required.
Knowledge of computer-aided design (CAD) systems is increasingly necessary.
job_outlook: The outlook is Moderate. Openings will largely come from the need to replace retiring workers in the manufacturing sector. Those with tech-forward skills (digital grading) will have a competitive edge.
labour_market_demand: The demand is focused on the apparel and leather manufacturing industries. With a shift back toward domestic "Made in Canada" production, skilled patternmakers who can work with both traditional methods and modern CAD software are in short supply.
High-Tech Manufacturing: Work with advanced pattern-making software (Gerber, Lectra).
Boutique Fashion Scene: Support emerging designers in luxury and bespoke markets.
Stable Employment: Consistent demand from established apparel manufacturers.
pathways: Express Entry (FSW/CEC): For patternmakers employed in the manufacturing sector.
OINP Employer Job Offer: Specifically for roles within Ontario's garment industry.
Morden Community Driven Immigration Initiative: For those targeting specific regional labor needs in Manitoba.
